channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Powerful US storms kill 2 and bring threats from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms killed two people in Mississippi, tore the roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in a small town in Oklahoma and threatened more communities across the nation Tuesday with wide-ranging weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Florida man swallows $769K in stolen jewelry from Tiffany & Co.: police
item.description: A man was accused of stealing hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of jewelry from a Tiffany & Co. store in Orlando and swallowing the evidence.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KTLA 5

item.title: Lutnick: Trump may announce tariff compromise with Canada, Mexico on Wednesday
item.description: Trump implementing 25 percent tariffs triggered retaliatory measures from both nations, with major corporations also warning that it could lead to potential price increases for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Judge tells attorneys to stop being so secretive in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An Idaho judge is warning attorneys to quit filing so many sealed documents in the murder case of a man accused in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former Los Angeles fire chief fails to get job back after ouster following historic deadly wildfire
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The former Los Angeles fire chief who was ousted by Mayor Karen Bass after the most destructive wildfire in city history failed to win back her job Tuesday, falling short with her argument that her dismissal was based on false accusations about her conduct and decision-making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Florida attorney general's office is investigating Andrew and Tristan Tate
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida's attorney general announced Tuesday that his office has opened a criminal investigation into Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, days after they returned to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The IRS is drafting plans to cut as much as half of its 90,000-person workforce, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS is drafting plans to cut its workforce by as much as half through a mix of layoffs, attrition and incentivized buyouts, according to two people familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump administration releases list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: The Trump administration has published a list of more than 440 federal properties it identified to close or sell, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.”.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jimmy John's is giving out free sandwiches. Here's how to redeem the limited-time deal
item.description: Sandwich chain Jimmy John’s is giving away $1 million worth of toasted subs on Tuesday to celebrate a new product launch.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Why Trump's speech to Congress isn't a 'State of the Union' address
item.description: It looks like the State of the Union, and will be carried on live television, just like those annual addresses are. But it's called something else: a joint address to Congress. And it has its origins in the first term of President Ronald Reagan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump wants to use the 'God Squad' to increase logging, but it must follow strict rules
item.description: President Donald Trump wants to increase logging in national forests and on public lands, including by bypassing endangered species protections.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ash Wednesday: What you should know about the start of the season
item.description: DAYTON, Ohio (WDTN) – What is Ash Wednesday? Ash Wednesday 2025, which falls on March 5 this year, marks the official beginning of the Christian season of Lent, which culminates with Easter on April 20, 2025. The 46-day season is meant for penitence, sacrifice, and reflection.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, Fox 5

item.title: Body of snowboarder missing from Vail Mountain found weeks later, coroner says
item.description: The Eagle County Coroner's Office on Monday confirmed in an email Gill's body was found Friday, two weeks after he was last seen.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: Fargo has an election method that helps mainstream candidates. North Dakota lawmakers may ban it
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — When Fargo voters approved a unique local election system in 2018, their goal was to choose candidates with broader support to lead North Dakota’s largest city. It worked — maybe too well.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mom of Highland Park shooter asserts son's innocence after courtroom outburst
item.description: "My son is innocent," Denise Pesina yelled. "He has no free will to make his own choices, and he has never had any outside help. They regulate everything. He has been a prisoner and this is wrong.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, NewsNation

item.title: IHOP offers free short stack for National Pancake Day
item.description: Calling all pancake lovers! If you are ready to satisfy your craving, IHOP is celebrating National Pancake Day on March 4 with a free treat.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, KRON4

item.title: Despite weather concerns, New Orleans celebrates Mardi Gras Day and bids farewell to Carnival season
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The good times and parades still rolled in New Orleans for Mardi Gras Day celebrations early Tuesday, ahead of expected severe winds, thunderstorms and tornado warnings in the afternoon.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hateful message left on Ohio restaurant receipt results in outpouring of support, staff says
item.description: The public is rallying behind a central Ohio Mexican restaurant after a customer left a hateful message instead of a tip.
item.dc:creator: Anna Hoffman, Fox 5

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts are finally closing in on their return to Earth after 9 months in space
item.description: NASA’s two stuck astronauts are just a few weeks away from finally returning to Earth after nine months in space.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Red Robin considering closing dozens of 'underperforming' restaurants
item.description: Red Robin, which first opened in Seattle in 1969, currently has 498 restaurants across the United States and in British Columbia, Canada.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, NewsNation

item.title: Red Robin considering closing dozens of 'underperforming' restaurants
item.description: Red Robin, which has locations in all but five states, says it's reviewing dozens of "underperforming" stores for potential closure.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: SDSU researchers ramp up border sewage, pollution survey
item.description: "About 70 percent of households reported some form of respiratory illness and the ones that reported smelling daily odors, or at least four to five times a week, had a higher response rate of having respiratory illnesses and gastrointestinal illnesses.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: FAA investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching DC airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said Monday it is investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge chooses not to sanction lawyer who admitted using AI in mistake-filled brief
item.description: U.S. District Judge Thomas Cullen will not sanction attorney Thomas Guyer for submitting a brief filled with incorrect citations and misquotes made by AI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon orders thousands more troops to southern border
item.description: The Pentagon is sending up to 5,050 additional troops to the U.S.-Mexico border even as unlawful border crossings have sharply fallen in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Powerful US storms kill 2 and bring threats from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: Powerful storms have killed two people in Mississippi, torn the roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in Oklahoma and threatened communities across the nation.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin And Jack Brook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How South Carolina's execution of a condemned killer by firing squad will unfold
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When a South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat steps into the death row chamber Friday night, it won’t be lethal injection or electrocution that ends his life.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered two new acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, in another setback to his battle to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Man pleads guilty to murder and attempted murder in shooting at suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill. (AP) — An Illinois man pleaded guilty Monday to killing seven people and injuring dozens more when he opened fire on a 2022 Independence Day parade in a Chicago suburb, a stunning development moments before opening statements in his trial on murder and attempted murder charges.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Storms, possible twisters to threaten the South just as New Orleans celebrates Mardi Gras
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms with a threat of tornadoes are expected to punch through Louisiana and other parts of the South on Tuesday just as costumed revelers celebrate Mardi Gras with huge parades and partying in the streets of New Orleans and other cities in the region.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Slain California fire captain's fugitive wife 'scared' ex mother-in-law
item.description: The former mother-in-law of a woman accused of murdering her fire captain wife in Southern California said that she was "very scary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Youngkin grants clemency to a fired Virginia police sergeant who shot and killed an unarmed man
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — Days after a judge sentenced a Virginia police officer to prison in the fatal shooting of an unarmed man accused of stealing sunglasses, the state's Republican governor granted the fired officer clemency, meaning he won't have to serve further time behind bars.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Killer whales amaze Seattle onlookers with a rarely seen bird hunt
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A pod of orcas swam close to shore and amazed onlookers in Seattle by treating the whale watchers to the rare sight of the apex predators hunting a bird.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California Highway Patrol blames drug use, speeding for fiery Cybertruck crash that killed 3
item.description: PIEDMONT, Calif. (AP) — Drug use and excessive speed were to blame for last year's fiery crash of a Tesla Cybertruck that killed three college students near San Francisco, the California Highway Patrol said in a preliminary report.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
